applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel with vectorized loads
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernel for 1D average pooling with: (1) 2D grid where each program handles one (batch, channel) pair and processes BLOCK_SIZE=1024 consecutive output elements, (2) vectorized loads using tl.arange for efficient memory access, (3) unrolled loop over kernel_size=8 positions, (4) proper handling of padding with bounds checking and zero-fill for out-of-bounds positions, (5) division by kernel_size (not valid count) to match PyTorch behavior

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE=64
attempted_but_failed.reason: Too many kernel launches, slower at 3.9ms

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE=2048
attempted_but_failed.reason: Slightly slower at 1.40ms vs 1.37ms for BLOCK_SIZE=1024

attempted_but_failed.name: Flat 1D grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: Similar performance, 2D grid with (batch*channel, blocks) was cleaner

top_bottlenecks: Memory bandwidth bound - each output element requires 8 input loads

notes: Key insight: PyTorch's AvgPool1d with padding adds zero-padding to input, then always divides by kernel_size (not by count of valid positions)
BLOCK_SIZE=1024 gave best results at 1.37ms (1.86x speedup over PyTorch reference at 2.55ms)
AMD wavefront size is 64, so BLOCK_SIZE should be multiple of 64 for best alignment
The kernel is memory-bandwidth bound - each output element requires loading 8 input values
Further optimization could explore: shared memory tiling for sliding window reuse, but stride=1 means adjacent outputs share 7/8 inputs

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output — agent stalled for the third consecutive time. The best score remains 68.60. With 48 minutes left, one more attempt with a complete copy-paste solution using sliding window

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent stalled for the 4th consecutive time producing no output. Providing a complete copy-paste solution with sliding window optimization to reduce memory loads from 8*BLOCK_SIZE to BLOCK_SIZE+7 per b
